analysis.summary: The technician logged into a local Windows 11 machine, then established a Remote Desktop Connection to a server at 10.10.1.10. On the remote server, the technician opened File Explorer and browsed through several standard user folders and the C: drive before disconnecting the RDP session and logging off the local machine.
actions_taken: Logged into local Windows 11 machine (user Quintana).
Initiated an RDP session to 10.10.1.10 and logged in as Administrator.
Opened File Explorer on the remote machine.
Navigated through C: drive, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, Pictures, Videos, and Music folders on the remote machine.
Closed File Explorer on the remote machine.
Signed out of the local Windows 11 machine.
notable_events: The local machine's lock screen time (07:07 AM) remained unchanged from the start to the end of the video, which might indicate a recording artifact or extremely short real-time duration of the session.
The remote machine's lock screen showed 11:24 PM, indicating a different time of day or timezone from the local machine.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician worked methodically, moving directly from login to establishing the RDP connection and then navigating folders without visible hesitation.
